established_in: Step 4.2 LETHALITY restoration + PP-233 formula correction

calibration.test_config: T3 mirror Line vs Line, P4 C4 D5 M6

entries.notes: Spec §3.1 stated initial value 0.10 with "iterate within 0.05-0.20" PROVISIONAL license. Calibrated value 1.25 is outside that range because PP-233 formula correction (continuous net_successes) raises baseline lethality from 3.96%/turn (discrete-bucket model at L=1.0) to 13.38%/turn (continuous at L=1.0). With the correct formula shape, LETHALITY_SCALE acts as fine-tune around ≈1.0 rather than gross compensation. See commit body for full diagnosis.

v17-v22 implemented as discrete DAMAGE_BY_DEGREE buckets {Overwhelming:1+p, Success:p, Partial:1, Failure:0}. PP-233 specifies continuous successes × (1+Power). Step 4.2 corrects implementation to match PP-233. DAMAGE_BY_DEGREE dict retained as narrative degree label for downstream logging only. DR retained as damage-reduction subtractor: PP-233 originally folded DR into H (Health per Size); v22 HP model uses Size × BLOCK_SIZE and drops H entirely, leaving DR-as-reduction as the only surviving DR effect.

HP = TroopCount = Size × BLOCK_SIZE. Company scale: Size 4 unit = 400 HP. Pre-existing constant; ledgered in Step 4.2 because LETHALITY_SCALE calibration depends on the HP scale this constant produces.
